summary: Generated and integrated a reusable Atlas Relay portal artwork as a premium command-hub panel with selected-lane stats and quick jumps to Path Map, Comms, Mission Feed, and system assets.

queries: game UI hub screen world map command center UX design pattern
dashboard visual hierarchy command center UX pattern
game progression map UI nodes unlocks hub design pattern
pattern_notes: The command hub should emphasize the selected lane and the next available action with clear visual hierarchy.
Dashboard panels should expose at-a-glance operational numbers before deeper drill-downs.
World-map and command-center references support a central visual anchor with routes, status, and navigational affordances.

image_generation.prompt_summary: Premium sci-fi fantasy system relay portal environment for an agent-company dashboard, wide 16:9, dark command chamber, luminous circular relay gate, floating map fragments, holographic routes, teal/cyan/gold palette, no text, no logos, no characters.

ui_contract: Render a generated Atlas Relay panel after the hero grid.
Use the selected lane to label the command route and derive the next objective text.
Show linked bot count, gate count, route event count, and artifact vault count.
Provide local-only PATH, COM, FEED, and ART actions.
PATH opens the selected lane Path Map, COM opens its command deck, FEED scrolls to the Mission Feed, and ART filters the Visual Asset Vault to system assets.
Register the relay artwork as a system asset in the Visual Asset Vault.

checks: 390x920 viewport: Atlas Relay rendered in one column, image loaded at 1672x941, no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.
1280x800 viewport: Atlas Relay rendered in two compact columns at 1236px wide and 391px tall with PATH, COM, FEED, and ART actions visible.
1280x800 interaction: PATH changed the hash to #lane=platform_engineering&view=path and rendered Path Map.
1280x800 interaction: COM changed the hash to #lane=platform_engineering&view=comms and rendered the command deck.
1280x800 interaction: ART filtered the Visual Asset Vault to system assets and showed exactly one Atlas relay portal card.
1600x900 viewport: Atlas Relay rendered in two columns with no horizontal overflow, no console errors, and no failed responses.
